items.title: One industry rules the roost in WA and neither side of politics can ignore it

children.content: Last week marked the first time the federal Liberal leader visited WA since he took the position, and during his trip west, both Angus Taylor and Premier Roger Cook were navigating the state's hot-button issues.

children.content: A 16-year-old boy has been charged for allegedly stabbing another teen, also 16, during a fight on a bus in Joondalup.

items.title: Green on form as Knight Riders win IPL thriller

items.title: Dingo destroyed after week of attacks in WA's north-west

children.content: A male dingo has been "humanely destroyed" after multiple attacks within days of each other at Western Australia's famous Karijini National Park.

image.altText: A dingo runs along a park fence, surrounded by yellow and green scrub.

items.title: Mother's anguish after daughter dies in prison cell

children.content: Deaths in custody are on the rise and family members and advocates for a woman who died in a Perth prison say more needs to be done to keep inmates safe.

image.altText: A woman sits at dusk holding a candle.

items.title: Fremantle Prison gains 'unique' documents about Catalpa captain

items.title: 'Membership to nothing': The multi-million-dollar Wild West of rewards clubs

children.content: "Rewards clubs" have exploded in Australia, claiming to have given away hundreds of millions in prizes, but they operate with almost no legal scrutiny.

image.altText: A large 4 wheel drive in a showroom clouded in smoke with dramatic red lighting.

items.title: An oil shock, a federal budget and the recession we may have to have

children.content: Accompanying the oil shock unfolding in the Strait of Hormuz is a profound change in the way fiscal and monetary policymakers operate. And as the government prepares the budget with the impact of the Iran war still unfolding, a recession may be ahead.

items.title: Trump turmoil is political kryptonite in Australia

children.content: As Donald Trump's approval in Australia sinks, Anthony Albanese needs to walk a careful line: keep voters convinced of the merits of the alliance, while avoiding the impression he is being too reverential.

items.title: US allies turning to Japan for defence supplies as Iran war drags on

children.content: Australia's multi-billion-dollar deal with Japan for its future warships could set a template that will help Tokyo rapidly expand defence exports across Asia.

items.title: Why the bedroom is an 'invisible workplace' for women

items.title: Leaders condemn 'disgusting' disruption by 'cretins' at WA Anzac Day services

articles.title: Veteran's advice after Anzac Day booing: Find your 'warrior spirit'

children.content: While condemnation has been swift after Indigenous elders were booed on Anzac Day, solutions to this type of racism seem harder to pin down. Elders have advice to share.
